_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: If the probability of an event A is P(A) = 0.3, what is the probability of the complementary event?
author_rationale: P(A^c) = 1 - P(A) = 0.7. The author did not notice that the three incorrect options all fall outside the interval [0,1], so they can be discarded without using the concept of a complement at all.

intended_defect.description: The three distractors (3, -0.3 and 1.3) are impossible values for a probability. A student can land on the right answer by arithmetic elimination without understanding the complement, so the item does not discriminate.
intended_defect.expected_finding: The distractor reviewer must report that none of the three distractors captures a plausible conceptual error about the complement, since all of them violate 0 ≤ P ≤ 1. A plausible distractor would be 0.3 (confusing the event with its complement).
